description: Add E2E tests for the plugin update UI lifecycle: update available dot indicator, clicking 'Update to vX.Y.Z' menu item, loading state during update, success feedback (version change, dot disappears), and failure error display.

userStories.title: E2E test: update available indicator and update menu item visibility
userStories.description: Test that when a plugin has an available update, the three-dot menu shows a yellow dot indicator and an 'Update to vX.Y.Z' menu item appears.
acceptanceCriteria: Plugin card three-dot menu button shows yellow update dot when update is available
Opening the menu shows 'Update to vX.Y.Z' item
When no update is available, no dot is shown and no Update menu item appears
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-plugin-update.e2e.ts (new file)

Context:
PluginMenu.tsx line 46-48:
```tsx
{plugin.update && (
  <div className="absolute top-0 right-0 h-1.5 w-1.5 rounded-full border border-background bg-primary" />
)}
```

PluginMenu.tsx lines 52-56:
```tsx
{plugin.update && (
  <Menu.Item onClick={onUpdate}>
    {updating ? <Loader size="sm" /> : <ArrowUpCircle className="h-3.5 w-3.5" />}
    Update to v{plugin.update.latestVersion}
  </Menu.Item>
)}
```

The `plugin.update` object comes from `plugin.checkUpdates` which the server runs periodically. For E2E testing, we need to set up a scenario where an installed plugin has a newer version available. The simplest approach:

1. Install a plugin via NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX to a temp dir
2. After install, the server's `checkUpdates` mechanism compares installed vs latest
3. If the installed version is older than npm's latest, `plugin.update` is populated

Alternatively, mock the update state by:
- Using a local plugin (which won't have npm updates) and patching the server state — too invasive
- Using the e2e-test plugin which is local and won't show updates

The most reliable approach: install `opentabs-plugin-slack` via NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX at a pinned older version (using `npm install -g opentabs-plugin-slack@0.0.50`), then trigger `plugin.checkUpdates`. If the published latest is newer, the update indicator appears.

This test is network-dependent and slow. Mark `test.slow()`.

Note: if no published plugin has a newer version available, this test cannot verify the update dot. Consider an alternative: manually inject `plugin.update` into the server state via a test-only endpoint or by having the e2e-test plugin report an update. Actually, the simplest approach may be to check the `checkUpdates` response first — if it returns outdated plugins, proceed with the UI test; if not, skip.

Constraints:
- Use NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX for install isolation
- Mark test.slow()
- This test may be flaky if no published plugin has a newer version — add a skip condition

userStories.title: E2E test: clicking Update triggers update and reflects in UI
userStories.description: Test clicking the 'Update to vX.Y.Z' menu item triggers the update, shows a loading state, and after completion the version changes and the update dot disappears.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Update to vX.Y.Z' menu item triggers the update
After update completes, the plugin version changes in the card
The update dot disappears from the menu button
The Update menu item no longer appears
If update fails, an error alert appears on the plugin card
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-plugin-update.e2e.ts (same file)

Context:
App.tsx `handleUpdatePlugin` calls `updatePlugin(pluginName)` from bridge.ts, which sends `bg:updatePlugin` → background → WebSocket → server `plugin.updateFromRegistry` → `npm update -g` → rediscovery.

On success, the server sends `plugins.changed` → side panel calls `getFullState()` → plugin now has the new version and `update` is undefined.

On failure, App.tsx sets `pluginErrors.set(pluginName, errorMessage)` → PluginCard renders an Alert with the error.

This test depends on US-001 having successfully identified an updatable plugin. It can be structured as a continuation of US-001 or as a combined test.

Constraints:
- This test requires a real npm update, which is slow (~10s)
- NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX must be set
- After update, verify via both the UI (version text, no dot) and the API (/health endpoint version field)
